subject: UX & Research
title: The Paradox of the File System
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Paradox of the File System.

hook.text: A 2022 study found that many Gen Z students don't understand folder structures. They search for everything instead.

buildup.text: File hierarchies mimic physical filing cabinets. But younger users never used cabinets — they grew up with search.

discovery.text: Google Drive and Spotlight trained a generation to find by query, not by navigating folders and subfolders.

twist.text: Professors report students saving all files to the desktop, never organizing. Search makes flat piles work.

climax.text: Information architecture is shifting from 'put things in the right place' to 'make things findable from anywhere.'

punchline.text: Folders are fading. Findability is replacing organization.

quiz.question: Why don't many younger users understand folder structures?
options: They grew up with search-based tools and never used physical filing systems
Modern computers don't support folders anymore
